title: inula helenium subsp. orgyalis
intro: Inula helenium subsp. orgyalis, also known as Elecampane, is a perennial plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its tall stem and large, white flowers. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Inula helenium subsp. orgyalis include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate skin conditions. Inula helenium subsp. orgyalis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in traditional herbal medicine to promote overall well-being.
